articles.title: A U.S. military blockade of Iran's ports takes effect. What does that mean?
articles.summary: A U.S. blockade of Iranian ports has gone into effect Monday, while President Trump claims Iran wants to make a deal.

articles.title: Many Popes Before Leo Have Been Entangled in Politics. Here Are 5 of Them.
articles.summary: Leo XIV, under fire from President Trump, has joined predecessors like Francis and John Paul II in confronting political issues.

articles.title: Trump’s Erratic Behavior and Extreme Comments Revive Mental Health Debate
articles.summary: As the president threatens to wipe out Iran and attacks the pope, even some former allies and advisers are questioning whether he has grown increasingly unbalanced, describing him as “lunatic” and “cl

articles.title: How Orban Lost His Touch, and Hungary’s Election
articles.summary: The election defeat for Prime Minister Viktor Orban is less the result of an ideological shift in Hungary, and more the playing out of a fundamental rule of politics.

articles.title: Trump’s Explanation for an Image of Himself as Jesus: ‘I Thought It Was Me as a Doctor’
articles.summary: The image showed President Trump bathed in divine light and clad in religious robes. His interpretation was that the image depicted him as a doctor, not Jesus Christ.

articles.title: Iran Blockade Sets Up a Test of Which Side Can Endure More Pain
articles.summary: President Trump is trying to choke off the country’s lifeline with a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. But the Iranians are betting that his tolerance for political pain is limited.

articles.title: Israelis Don’t Feel Much Like Victors in War With Iran
articles.summary: The regime in Iran has not changed and the nuclear and missile threats have not been eliminated, leaving many Israelis to wonder what this was all for.

articles.title: Pride Flag Can Fly at Stonewall After Trump Administration Reversal
articles.summary: The settlement ends a symbolic attack on the L.G.B.T.Q. rights movement and deals a blow to the administration’s assault on diversity initiatives.

articles.title: 5 things to know about Péter Magyar, Hungary's new prime minister
articles.summary: Magyar ended Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's 16-year grip on power in a landslide victory on Sunday. The former Orbán loyalist burst onto the scene as an opposition leader in 2024.

articles.title: To Open the Strait of Hormuz, Trump Wants to Blockade Iran. Experts Are Skeptical.
articles.summary: President Trump’s announcement of a U.S. blockade was his latest effort to pressure Iran to negotiate after direct talks over the weekend did not yield an agreement.

articles.title: Judge dismisses Trump's $10B lawsuit over the Wall Street Journal's Epstein reporting
articles.summary: In the order issued Monday, the judge wrote that President Trump had failed to make the argument that the article, which described a letter to Epstein that the newspaper said bore Trump's signature, w

articles.title: Trump says U.S. will blockade Iranian ports. And, Orbán loses Hungarian election
articles.summary: President Trump announced a blockade of Iranian ports after peace talks with Iran collapsed. And, Viktor Orbán concedes defeat after 16 years in power in Hungary.

articles.title: Trump vows to sink Iranian ships approaching a U.S. blockade of Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: President Trump said the U.S. would interdict vessels that had to pay what he called an "illegal toll" to Iran to cross the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Retired U.S. Navy admiral on Trump's threat to blockade the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: NPR's Michel Martin speaks to retired U.S. Navy Adm. James Foggo, dean of the Center for Maritime Strategy, about President Trump's command to blockade the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Pope Leo brushes off Trump criticism amid growing Vatican–U.S. tensions over Iran war
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV says he will not be deterred by criticism from President Trump, vowing to continue his calls for peace as tensions escalate between the Vatican and Washington over the Iran conflict.

articles.title: What a chimpanzee 'civil war' can teach us about how societies fall apart
articles.summary: A long-term study of the world's largest known community of chimpanzees has documented a rare event: what the researchers describe as the primate equivalent of a "civil war."

articles.title: Morning news brief
articles.summary: President Trump says U.S. will blockade the Strait of Hormuz, failed U.S.-Iran negotiations cause oil prices to surge, Hungary's Viktor Orbán loses reelection bid.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
